name: Cream of Wheat Crepes with Sesame Potatoes&
recipeIngredient: 2/3 cup Quick cream of wheat cereal
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the cream of wheat and flour in a bowl. Whisk in olive oil and 2 cups water. Cover and set aside.
2. For the potato filling, pan-toast sesame seeds in a skillet until they jump. Stir in jalapeno peppers and mashed potatoes, then fry for 3 minutes. Sprinkle with lime juice and season with salt and pepper. Toss to mix and remove from heat. Spray with oil.
3. Place mustard seeds in a small pan, cover, and toast. Pour into the crepe batter when popped. Add salt and baking powder. The batter should be thin; add water as necessary.
4. Heat crepe pans and spray with oil. Pour 1/3 cup of batter into each pan to form a 10-inch crepe. Cook for 3-4 minutes, then flip and cook for another 1-2 minutes. Repeat with remaining batter. Stack crepes between waxed paper when cool.
5. Preheat the oven to 375°F. Line a shallow oven-proof dish with parchment paper and spray with oil. Place each crepe on a work surface and spoon 1/3 cup of potato mixture into the center. Roll logs and place on a baking sheet. Spray with oil and bake for 10-12 minutes.
6. Serve two crepes on a warmed plate, spooning green pea chutney down the middle.
Creamy, savory crepes topped with spiced potatoes and finished with a zesty almond-pea chutney.
